questions.question: Which Notion plans allow you to connect third‑party apps to Notion AI?

options.text: Free plan only
options.explanation: Only the Business or Enterprise plans support third‑party connections; the Free plan does not.

options.text: Business or Enterprise plans
options.explanation: The article states that you must be on the Business or Enterprise plan to connect third‑party apps.

options.text: All paid plans, including Personal Pro
options.explanation: Personal Pro is not mentioned as sufficient; only Business and Enterprise are allowed.

options.text: Only Enterprise plan
options.explanation: Business plan also grants this capability, not just Enterprise.

questions.question: Where can you search content from connected apps using Notion AI Connectors?

options.text: Only in the Notion AI chat window
options.explanation: Search is also possible elsewhere, not just the chat window.

options.text: In the Notion AI chat, the AI sidebar, and the regular sidebar search
options.explanation: The article lists all three locations as searchable spots.

options.text: Only in the Notion AI sidebar
options.explanation: Search is also available in the chat and regular sidebar.

options.text: Only via the Settings → Notion AI page
options.explanation: That page is for managing connectors, not for searching content.

questions.question: What role must you have in a third‑party app to successfully set up its Notion AI Connector?

options.text: Viewer access
options.explanation: Viewer rights are insufficient; admin rights are required.

options.text: Editor access
options.explanation: Editor rights may allow content changes but not the permissions needed for connector setup.

options.text: Admin rights in the third‑party app
options.explanation: The article explicitly says you need admin rights in the app you’re connecting.

options.text: Any role, as long as you’re the Notion workspace owner
options.explanation: Both workspace ownership and app admin rights are required.

questions.question: If the workspace owner who installed a connector leaves the organization, what happens to the connector?

options.text: The connector is automatically disabled
options.explanation: The connector continues to work; it is not automatically disabled.

options.text: It remains active until a new owner disconnects it
options.explanation: The article states the connector keeps working as is after the owner leaves.

options.text: Notion prompts the next admin to reinstall it
options.explanation: No such prompt is mentioned.

options.text: All data from that connector is immediately deleted
options.explanation: Data persists; only a manual disconnect removes it.

questions.question: Which connector has a unique behavior where Notion will automatically find another workspace owner to re‑establish the connection if the original owner leaves?

options.text: Slack
options.explanation: Slack does not have this auto‑re‑establish feature.

options.text: Google Drive
options.explanation: The article notes Google Drive is the only exception with automatic reassignment.

options.text: Gmail
options.explanation: Gmail is not mentioned as having this behavior.

options.text: Notion Mail
options.explanation: Notion Mail does not involve workspace‑owner reassignment.

questions.question: What is the typical maximum time span of historical data that a Notion AI Connector can access after it is set up?

options.text: Six months prior to the connection date
options.explanation: The article specifies a one‑year window, not six months.

options.text: All historical data, regardless of age
options.explanation: Access is limited; it does not include data older than a year.

options.text: One year prior to the setup date
options.explanation: The article states connectors can access information dating back one year from the setup date.

options.text: Only data created after the connector is installed
options.explanation: Historical data up to one year is also searchable.

questions.question: Which of the following statements best describes the type of tasks Notion AI Connectors are designed for?

options.text: Running complex statistical analyses across multiple data sources
options.explanation: The article explicitly says connectors are not meant for complex calculations or data analysis.

options.text: Finding and summarizing information from connected apps
options.explanation: Connectors excel at locating and summarizing content, as highlighted in the best‑practice section.

options.text: Automatically editing documents based on AI‑generated insights
options.explanation: Editing documents is not a primary purpose of connectors.

options.text: Generating new content in third‑party apps without user prompts
options.explanation: Connectors only surface existing content; they do not generate new content in external apps.

questions.question: If you disconnect a connector, how long can it take for your data to become unsearchable and then be deleted?

options.text: Data becomes unsearchable immediately and is deleted after 24 hours
options.explanation: The article says unsearchable can take up to one hour, and deletion occurs within a day.

options.text: Data remains searchable for 72 hours and is deleted after a week
options.explanation: Those timeframes are not mentioned for disconnection.

options.text: Data is instantly deleted and becomes unsearchable after 48 hours
options.explanation: Deletion is not instant; unsearchability can be up to one hour.

options.text: Data stays searchable for 30 days before any deletion occurs
options.explanation: The article specifies a much shorter window.
